Our Programs

Two programs — both rooted in the living tradition of Sanskrit

✦
APRIL 15 – MAY 15
Shloka Patha
श्लोकपाठ
30 days of daily shloka learning — from daily prayers to Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12, Subhashitas and Ramayana. One hour every day, Monday to Saturday. Certificate for every participant.
₹1,200
per student · 10 seats only · certificate included
  • 26 live sessions · Mon–Sat · 1 hr each
  • Week 1 — Daily shlokas with meaning
  • Week 2 — Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12 (Bhakti Yoga)
  • Week 3 — Subhashitas — Sanskrit wise sayings
  • Week 4 — Ramayana shlokas + competition
  • 15-min revision every session
  • Certificate for all participants
  • WhatsApp group + all resources free

MAY 2026
Sanskrit Summer Camp
ग्रीष्मकालीन शिविर
An intensive 4-week live online camp. Script, reading, writing, shlokas and stories — a complete Sanskrit foundation in one summer.
₹2,000
per student · 4 weeks · certificate included
  • 4-week structured curriculum
  • Devanagari script & pronunciation
  • Bhagavad Gita selected shlokas
  • Subhashitas with word-by-word meaning

  • Recorded sessions access
  • Completion certificate
  • Only 10 seats — limited batch
Register for Summer Camp

Curriculum

Shloka Patha — 4-Week Syllabus

From daily prayers to Bhagavad Gita, Subhashitas and Ramayana — no prior knowledge needed

✦

Week 1 · Apr 15–20

Daily Shlokas

  • Guru Shloka — गुरुर्ब्रह्मा
  • Saraswati Vandana — या कुन्देन्दु
  • Shubha Prabhata — प्रातः स्मरामि
  • Shanti Patha — ॐ सह नाववतु
  • Ganesha & Bhumi Vandana shlokas
  • Pronunciation + word-by-word meaning

Week 2 · Apr 21–27

Bhagavad Gita Ch. 12

  • Bhakti Yoga — the yoga of devotion
  • All 20 shlokas of Chapter 12
  • Word-by-word breakdown each session
  • Revision of previous day's shloka daily
  • Understanding bhakti through Krishna's words
  • Recitation practice with correct rhythm

Week 3 · Apr 28–May 4

Subhashitas

  • What are subhashitas — wise sayings
  • 6 selected subhashitas with deep meaning
  • Topics — knowledge, friendship, effort, truth
  • Grammar and vocabulary hidden in each
  • Writing + recitation practice daily
  • Students pick their favourite subhashita

Week 4 · May 5–15

Ramayana + Competition

  • Selected shlokas from Valmiki Ramayana
  • Rama's character through shlokas
  • Shloka recitation competition — all participate
  • Grand revision of all 4 weeks
  • Certificate ceremony — every student honoured
  • Showcase session — parents invited

Our mission

About Daivivak

Reviving the divine language for the next generation

✦
Shreeshanth Yajurvedi
Sanskrit & Veda Adhyayi · Founder, Daivivak

Namasте! I am Shreeshanth Yajurvedi — a student of Sanskrit and the Vedas, and a passionate teacher who believes that this sacred language belongs in every home, every heart, and every generation.

Sanskrit and the Vedas are not just subjects I study — they are my parampara, my living tradition passed down through my lineage. For the past 3 years, I have been taking daily Sanskrit classes for students at my home, walking them through the beauty of this ancient language step by step, from the very first akshara to the verses of the Bhagavad Gita.

Daivivak — meaning "The Divine Voice" — was born from a simple belief: every child deserves the chance to hear, speak, and understand the language of the Vedas. Through this summer camp, I bring that same warmth, discipline, and devotion that my students at home experience every day — now online, for young minds across India.

  • Student of Sanskrit and the Vedas — rooted in parampara
  • 3 years of daily teaching experience — home classes
  • Specialisation in Vedic chanting, Bhagavad Gita & subhashitas
  • Teaching ages 10–16 with a structured, story-led approach
  • Founder, Daivivak — dedicated to Sanskrit education
